content.problemStatement:
You are given an NГ—NN\times NNГ—N square matrix GGG. Each cell of the matrix contains either `O` or `X `.

A movement from cell (X1,Y1)(X_1, Y_1)(X1вЂ‹,Y1вЂ‹) to (X2,Y2)(X_2, Y_2)(X2вЂ‹,Y2вЂ‹) is legal only if:


G(X1,Y1)=G(X2,Y2)=G_{(X_1,Y_1)}=G_{(X_2,Y_2)}=G(X1вЂ‹,Y1вЂ‹)вЂ‹=G(X2вЂ‹,Y2вЂ‹)вЂ‹= `O`;
X1=X2X_1=X_2X1вЂ‹=X2вЂ‹ or Y1=Y2Y_1=Y_2Y1вЂ‹=Y2вЂ‹.
A path (X1,Y1)в†’(X2,Y2)в†’в‹Їв†’(XK,YK)(X_1,Y_1)\rightarrow (X_2,Y_2)\rightarrow \dots \rightarrow (X_{K},Y_{K})(X1вЂ‹,Y1вЂ‹)в†’(X2вЂ‹,Y2вЂ‹)в†’в‹Їв†’(XKвЂ‹,YKвЂ‹) of **length** KKK is valid if it meets **all** of the following conditions:


It starts from the first row and ends at the first row. Formally, X1=XK=1X_1=X_{K}=1X1вЂ‹=XKвЂ‹=1.
You can **not** visit a cell **twice**. Formally, for any pair (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that (1в‰¤i<jв‰¤K)(1\le i\lt j \le K)(1в‰¤i<jв‰¤K), either Xiв‰ XjX_i \neq X_jXiвЂ‹оЂ =XjвЂ‹ or Yiв‰ YjY_i \neq Y_jYiвЂ‹оЂ =YjвЂ‹ holds true .
(Xi,Yi)в†’(X(i+1),Y(i+1))(X_i, Y_i)\rightarrow (X_{(i+1)}, Y_{(i+1)})(XiвЂ‹,YiвЂ‹)в†’(X(i+1)вЂ‹,Y(i+1)вЂ‹) is a legal movement for all (1в‰¤i<K)(1\le i < K)(1в‰¤i<K).
**At least** one cell outside the first row is visited. Formally, в€ѓXiв‰ 1\exist X_i \neq 1в€ѓXiвЂ‹оЂ =1 (1<i<K)(1<i<K)(1<i<K).
You need to answer QQQ queries. Each query is of the form:


XXX YYY: Given integers XXX and YYY, such that (1в‰¤X,Yв‰¤N)(1\le X, Y\le N)(1в‰¤X,Yв‰¤N) and G(X,Y)=G_{(X,Y)} = G(X,Y)вЂ‹= `O`, change G(X,Y)G_{(X,Y)}G(X,Y)вЂ‹ to `X` and find the **length** of the **shortest** valid path in the matrix. If no such path exists, print в€’1-1в€’1 instead.
Note: Each modification is **independent**. In other words, for each query, we modify on the original matrix GGG.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ, the dimension of matrix and the number of queries.
The next NNN lines describe GGG. The ithi^{th}ith of these NNN lines contains NNN characters.
The next QQQ lines describe the queries. For the ithi^{th}ith line, there are two space-separated integers XiX_iXiвЂ‹ and YiY_iYiвЂ‹, describing the ithi^{th}ith query.

Note: It is guaranteed that before each modification G(Xi,Yi)=G_{(X_i,Y_i)} =G(XiвЂ‹,YiвЂ‹)вЂ‹= `O`, and, there are no duplicate queries.



Output Format
For each query, output on a new line, the **length** of the **shortest** valid path in the matrix. If no such path exists, print в€’1-1в€’1 instead.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Query 111: Update G(3,3)G_{(3, 3)}G(3,3)вЂ‹ to X. The smallest valid path is (1,1)в†’(3,1)в†’(3,2)в†’(1,2)(1,1)\rightarrow (3,1)\rightarrow (3,2)\rightarrow (1,2)(1,1)в†’(3,1)в†’(3,2)в†’(1,2).

Query 222: Update G(3,1)G_{(3, 1)}G(3,1)вЂ‹ to X. The smallest valid path is (1,1)в†’(2,1)в†’(2,3)в†’(3,3)в†’(3,2)в†’(1,2)(1,1)\rightarrow (2,1)\rightarrow (2,3)\rightarrow (3,3)\rightarrow (3,2)\rightarrow (1,2)(1,1)в†’(2,1)в†’(2,3)в†’(3,3)в†’(3,2)в†’(1,2).

Query 333: Update G(1,1)G_{(1, 1)}G(1,1)вЂ‹ to X. No valid path exists.
